tasks.subject: Task 1: M2 — minimum-entropy check in validateAdminSecret
tasks.description:
**Goal:** validateAdminSecret rejects long-but-trivially-weak secrets ('a'.repeat(32)) while still accepting any real random secret incl. the deployed 64-hex value.

**Files:**
- Modify: server/validateAdminSecret.js
- Test: server/admin-secret.test.js

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- <32/non-string -> 'must be set and at least 32 characters'
- >=32 but <5 distinct chars -> 'too weak (needs at least 5 distinct characters)'
- real 64-hex and >=5-distinct -> null
- old 'a'*32->null case REPLACED with rejection
- full suite green

**Verify:** npx jest server/admin-secret.test.js -v && npx jest --silent

**Steps:** TDD — rewrite test (replace the 'a'.repeat(32)->null case with two distinct-char rejection cases + a real 64-hex acceptance); add `new Set(secret).size < 5` branch with a distinct message after the length check; run; commit.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["server/validateAdminSecret.js", "server/admin-secret.test.js"], "verifyCommand": "npx jest server/admin-secret.test.js -v && npx jest --silent", "acceptanceCriteria": ["short/non-string -> /at least 32/", ">=32 with <5 distinct -> /distinct characters/", "real 64-hex + >=5-distinct -> null", "old 'a'*32->null replaced with rejection", "full suite green"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: M3 — Redis-backed shared store for adminLimiter
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Global 5/15min/IP admin ceiling across instances + deploys, Redis-backed, fail-open on store error, still unit-testable without booting server.js.

**Files:**
- Modify: package.json (add rate-limit-redis)
- Modify: server/adminLimiter.js (singleton -> createAdminLimiter(redisClient) factory + failOpen wrapper)
- Modify: server.js (relocate pubClient/subClient construction above the admin-limiter mount; mount via factory)
- Test: server/admin-ratelimit.test.js

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- createAdminLimiter(redisClient) factory returns express middleware
- rate-limit-redis store whose sendCommand delegates to injected client as args array
- store errors fail OPEN (served, never 500)
- 5 ok then 6th 429 preserved; auth failures consume budget
- pubClient declared before mount, mount before /api/admin routes
- rate-limit-redis in deps; full suite green

**Verify:** npx jest server/admin-ratelimit.test.js -v && npx jest --silent

**Steps:** npm i rate-limit-redis@^4; TDD test with jest.mock('rate-limit-redis') in-memory Store double (asserts factory shape, sendCommand delegation, 5->429, fail-open); implement factory + failOpen wrapper; rewire server.js (move pubClient/subClient block above line 106; change mount to createAdminLimiter(pubClient)); run; commit. NOTE: verify exact express-rate-limit@8 Store method set for the failOpen wrapper during red->green.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["package.json", "server/adminLimiter.js", "server.js", "server/admin-ratelimit.test.js"], "verifyCommand": "npx jest server/admin-ratelimit.test.js -v && npx jest --silent", "acceptanceCriteria": ["createAdminLimiter(redisClient) factory", "store sendCommand delegates to injected client as args array", "store error fails open (never 500)", "5 ok then 6th 429 preserved", "pubClient before mount, mount before routes", "rate-limit-redis in deps", "full suite green"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: R1 — route the two non-current-turn branches through withLobbyLock
tasks.description:
**Goal:** quitGame's and the grace-timer's non-current-turn branches do their read-modify-write inside withLobbyLock; broadcast outside on the returned room.

**Files:**
- Modify: server/systems/lobbySystem.js (quitGame ~633-642; handleDisconnect grace timer ~735-741)
- Test: server/quit-grace-lock.test.js (new)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- quitGame non-current branch mutates only inside withLobbyLock; emits/checkWinCondition/broadcast after the lock on the returned room
- grace-timer non-current branch likewise, re-finding player by stableId/id on the fresh in-lock room
- two concurrent non-current quits both persist (no lost update)
- already-dead/absent player -> mutator returns false -> no spurious persist
- current-turn branches unchanged
- full suite green

**Verify:** npx jest server/quit-grace-lock.test.js -v && npx jest --silent

**Steps:** TDD — new test reusing lobby-lock.test.js fake-redis double driving two concurrent non-current quits (fails: resurrection); wrap both else-branches in withLobbyLock(mutator returns false if gone/dead), broadcast outside; run; commit.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["server/systems/lobbySystem.js", "server/quit-grace-lock.test.js"], "verifyCommand": "npx jest server/quit-grace-lock.test.js -v && npx jest --silent", "acceptanceCriteria": ["quitGame non-current branch mutates inside withLobbyLock", "grace-timer non-current branch mutates inside withLobbyLock re-find by stableId/id", "two concurrent non-current quits both persist", "already-dead -> mutator false, no resurrection", "current-turn branches unchanged", "full suite green"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: R2 — periodic watchdog re-arm sweep
tasks.description:
**Goal:** New gameLogic.sweepMissingTurnWatchdogs arms a watchdog for any playing lobby this instance isn't watching; .unref()'d 30s interval in server.js runs it.

**Files:**
- Modify: server/gameLogic.js (add + export sweepMissingTurnWatchdogs after recoverActiveTurns)
- Modify: server.js (TURN_SWEEP_INTERVAL_MS=30000 + .unref()'d interval after the boot recovery block)
- Test: server/turn-sweep.test.js (new)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- sweepMissingTurnWatchdogs(io, pubClient) exported
- arms only playing lobbies with !hasActiveTurnTimeout(id), returns count
- does NOT reset a healthy timer; skips non-playing
- getAllLobbies rejection swallowed -> returns 0
- server.js 30s .unref() interval
- full suite green

**Verify:** npx jest server/turn-sweep.test.js -v && npx jest --silent

**Steps:** TDD — new test (jest.mock redisUtils): arms playing+unwatched; does NOT re-arm pre-armed; skips waiting; swallows rejection. Implement function gating on !hasActiveTurnTimeout, export it, add server.js constant + .unref()'d interval. blockedBy Task 2 (both edit server.js — serialize for clean patches).

```json:metadata
{"files": ["server/gameLogic.js", "server.js", "server/turn-sweep.test.js"], "verifyCommand": "npx jest server/turn-sweep.test.js -v && npx jest --silent", "acceptanceCriteria": ["sweepMissingTurnWatchdogs exported", "arms only playing + !hasActiveTurnTimeout, returns count", "does not reset healthy timer", "skips non-playing", "swallows getAllLobbies rejection -> 0", "server.js 30s .unref() interval", "full suite green"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: R3 — eliminate the Jest leaked-handle warning
tasks.description:
**Goal:** npx jest and npx jest --coverage exit clean (no worker-exit / open-handle warning), suite green, incl. R2's new interval.

**Files:**
- Modify: server.js (.unref() best-effort module/boot intervals as diagnosis dictates)
- Modify: whichever test files --detectOpenHandles implicates (afterAll/afterEach teardown)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- --detectOpenHandles --runInBand reports no open handle
- jest prints no worker-exit warning, suite green
- jest --coverage exits clean
- no production behavior change

**Verify:** npx jest --detectOpenHandles --runInBand 2>&1 | tail -40 && npx jest 2>&1 | tail -5

**Steps:** Run --detectOpenHandles; for each handle apply the fixed-shape fix (.unref() the poster-refresh + leaderboard-prune + R2 intervals; add clearTurnTimeout/afterAll teardown to implicated tests). If nothing reported post-Task-4, record that and acceptance is verification-only. blockedBy Task 4 (clean-exit must cover R2's interval).

```json:metadata
{"files": ["server.js"], "verifyCommand": "npx jest --detectOpenHandles --runInBand 2>&1 | tail -40 && npx jest 2>&1 | tail -5", "acceptanceCriteria": ["--detectOpenHandles reports no open handle", "jest no worker-exit warning, suite green", "jest --coverage exits clean", "no production behavior change"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 6: CI2 — core happy-path test + coverage ratchet
tasks.description:
**Goal:** submitMovie->commitPlay success path has a regression test; jest.config.js enforces a coverageThreshold at the measured ratchet floor.

**Files:**
- Test: server/systems/matchSystem.test.js (append success-path describe)
- Modify: jest.config.js (top-level collectCoverageFrom + coverageThreshold)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- test: valid movie sharing an actor with chain head -> committed, turn advances, not eliminated/rejected, isValidating released
- jest.config collectCoverageFrom server/**/*.js excl tests
- coverageThreshold.global set 1pt below measured post-Phase-2 coverage
- threshold gate verified to fail when coverage drops (scratch, not committed)
- suite green + clean exit

**Verify:** npx jest server/systems/matchSystem.test.js -t "commits a valid play" -v && npx jest --coverage --silent

**Steps:** Append happy-path test mirroring the H3 harness (direct-tmdbId path, cast shares Leonardo DiCaprio with seeded Inception chain head, real gameLogic, clearTurnTimeout afterEach); run; measure coverage; set thresholds to floor(measured)-1 (concrete integers, not tokens); verify gate fails when bumped; commit. blockedBy Task 5 (measure on a clean-exiting suite with all new tests present).

```json:metadata
{"files": ["server/systems/matchSystem.test.js", "jest.config.js"], "verifyCommand": "npx jest server/systems/matchSystem.test.js -t \"commits a valid play\" -v && npx jest --coverage --silent", "acceptanceCriteria": ["happy-path: valid play not eliminated/rejected, turn advances, isValidating released", "collectCoverageFrom server/**/*.js excl tests", "coverageThreshold.global 1pt below measured", "gate verified to fail when coverage drops", "suite green + clean exit"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 7: CI1 — GitHub Actions workflow
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Every push/PR to main runs npm ci + the full suite with coverage on a Node 18/20 matrix, gating the Render auto-deploy.

**Files:**
- Create: .github/workflows/ci.yml

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- valid YAML, triggers on push + pull_request to main
- npm ci then npm test -- --coverage --runInBand on Node 18 and 20
- no secrets required (Redis doubles/mocks)
- green Actions run after push

**Verify:** node -e "require('js-yaml').load(require('fs').readFileSync('.github/workflows/ci.yml','utf8'));console.log('OK')"

**Steps:** Create ci.yml (push+PR to main, Node 18/20 matrix, actions/checkout@v4 + setup-node@v4 cache npm, npm ci, npm test -- --coverage --runInBand); validate YAML locally; commit. Confirm green on GitHub after the PR push. blockedBy Task 6 (CI enforces the Task 6 threshold; must go green first run).

```json:metadata
{"files": [".github/workflows/ci.yml"], "verifyCommand": "node -e \"require('js-yaml').load(require('fs').readFileSync('.github/workflows/ci.yml','utf8'));console.log('OK')\"", "acceptanceCriteria": ["valid YAML, triggers push+PR to main", "npm ci + npm test --coverage --runInBand Node 18/20", "no secrets needed", "green Actions run after push"]}
```
